pred_reason: SWC-101 applies due to multiple integer overflow/underflow vulnerabilities identified in arithmetic operations using Solidity 0.6.12 without built-in protections. SWC-107 is appropriate because the contract exhibits reentrancy risks in the onSushiReward function, including cross-function reentrancy with malicious reward tokens and an ineffective lock modifier. Evidence: Integer overflow/underflow found in arithmetic operations using BoringMath without safeguards; Reentrancy vulnerability in onSushiReward with unsafe external call patterns; Ineffective lock modifier failing to prevent reentrancy attacks

raw.pred_reason: SWC-101 applies due to multiple integer overflow/underflow vulnerabilities identified in arithmetic operations using Solidity 0.6.12 without built-in protections. SWC-107 is appropriate because the contract exhibits reentrancy risks in the onSushiReward function, including cross-function reentrancy with malicious reward tokens and an ineffective lock modifier. Evidence: Integer overflow/underflow found in arithmetic operations using BoringMath without safeguards; Reentrancy vulnerability in onSushiReward with unsafe external call patterns; Ineffective lock modifier failing to prevent reentrancy attacks
